constraints.name: Aqueous Media Requirement
constraints.description: Transit-time ultrasonic measurement requires acoustic coupling between piezoelectric transducers and the fluid. Non-aqueous media (oils, gases, non-polar solvents, polymer melts) have acoustic impedance mismatches that prevent signal propagation. Water, water-glycol mixtures, and aqueous suspensions are supported.

constraints.name: Maximum Medium Temperature
constraints.description: Polymer housing and seals degrade above 90°C. Extended operation near limit reduces service life. Material constraint of the current product design, not a fundamental limit of transit-time technology.

constraints.name: Air Bubble / Void Fraction Limit
constraints.description: Air entrainment >2% by volume attenuates ultrasonic signal. Above ~5% void fraction, signal scatter prevents any usable transit-time measurement. Chronic high-void-fraction media require upstream de-aerators.

constraints.name: Temperature-Dependent Accuracy
constraints.description: Speed of sound changes with temperature. Uncompensated error ~1.5%. Built-in NTC compensation reduces to ~0.2% steady-state. Extreme transients may temporarily exceed compensation response.

constraints.name: Wetted Material Chemical Compatibility
constraints.description: Wetted parts (polymer housing, seals) approved for potable water contact (KTW/WRAS) but not rated for aggressive chemicals — concentrated acids, bases, oxidizers, or organic solvents would attack the housing. Alternative wetted materials are standard in the flowmeter industry but not in the current Marquardt product line.

constraints.name: Pipe Full Condition Required
constraints.description: Pipe must be completely filled with liquid. Partially filled pipes cause complete signal loss — ultrasonic pulse cannot traverse an air gap. Inherent to inline transit-time technology.

constraints.name: Minimum Flow Rate Detection
constraints.description: SNR degrades at very low flow rates. Lower detection limit varies by pipe diameter: ~0.25 l/min (DN14), ~4.2 l/min (DN20/DN25).

constraints.name: Maximum Operating Pressure
constraints.description: Housing rated to 10 bar continuous. Burst pressure 9 bar (low-pressure variant) or 42 bar (high-pressure variant). Higher-pressure variants are a standard design path.

constraints.name: Minimum Straight Pipe Run
constraints.description: Turbulent or asymmetric flow profiles from upstream disturbances degrade accuracy. 5-10 pipe diameters of straight run recommended upstream. Flow conditioners can reduce this requirement.

constraints.name: Life Endurance Ceiling
constraints.description: Total throughput endurance rated at 3,000 m³. At max flow (46.6 l/min DN20), this equates to ~45 days continuous. High-flow 24/7 applications exhaust sensor life quickly, making replacement cost uneconomical.

constraints.name: No Explosive Atmosphere Certification
constraints.description: No ATEX/IECEx certification for explosive atmospheres. Certification path exists via intrinsically safe (Ex-ia) barrier modules or Ex-d/Ex-e enclosure designs.

constraints.name: Ambient Temperature Range
constraints.description: Electronics and polymer housing designed for indoor/protected installation. RAST 2.5 connector is appliance-grade. Extreme ambient conditions may exceed electronics operating range. Heated/cooled enclosures are a standard mitigation.
